message:
fix(oauth1): use sorted Vec for parameter collection and OsRng for nonce generation

Fix two correctness issues in the OAuth1 signature base string construction:

1. Replace HashMap-based parameter collection with Vec<(String, String)> to
   preserve duplicate query parameters (per RFC 5849 Section 3.4.1.3.1, all
   parameters including duplicates must be included). The HashMap silently
   dropped duplicate keys, which could produce invalid signatures for URLs
   with repeated parameter names. Parameters are now explicitly sorted with
   sort_unstable() before building the parameter string.

2. Replace rand::random() byte-by-byte nonce generation with a single
   OsRng.fill_bytes() call, which is both more efficient (one syscall
   instead of 32) and more clearly communicates the cryptographic intent
   by using the OS-level CSPRNG directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
